changes.value:
## What

The LSP YAML files in `builtin/lsp/*.yaml` already define `command`, `file_extensions`, `install_hint`, and `project_types` per server. But they're missing an `icon` field (emoji for statusline), and the registry API (`servers_for_project`) only queries by project type ã not by file extension.

This card adds the missing pieces so the YAML-based registry becomes the single source of truth for language-to-LSP-to-icon mapping.

### Files to change:
- `swissarmyhammer-lsp/src/types.rs` ã add `icon: Option<String>` to `OwnedLspServerSpec`
- `builtin/lsp/*.yaml` ã add `icon` field to each (ÞÎ, Þ, Þ, Þ¿, Þ, Þ)
- Add YAML files for languages that the statusline currently hardcodes but have no YAML: Ruby (solargraph), Go (gopls), Java (jdtls), C# (omnisharp), C/C++ (clangd), Dart, Swift, Kotlin
- `swissarmyhammer-lsp/src/registry.rs` ã add `fn all_servers() -> &[OwnedLspServerSpec]` and `fn servers_for_extensions(exts: &[&str]) -> Vec<&OwnedLspServerSpec>`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every LSP YAML file has an `icon` field
- [ ] `OwnedLspServerSpec` includes `icon: Option<String>`
- [ ] `all_servers()` returns every loaded spec
- [ ] `servers_for_extensions(&["rs"])` returns rust-analyzer spec with icon
- [ ] YAML files exist for all languages the statusline currently shows icons for

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: `servers_for_extensions` returns correct specs for known extensions
- [ ] Unit test: `all_servers()` includes all YAML-loaded servers
- [ ] Unit test: loaded specs have non-empty icon field
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-lsp`
Add icon field to LSP YAML specs and expose extension-based registry query
